Discovery: Crashed 12.5% on April 29 despite Q1 2026 beat (41% YoY revenue growth to $1.1B) — sold off entirely on unchanged full-year guidance; management guides 38-42% EPS CAGR through 2028 and $0.60 adjusted EPS for FY2026; post-earnings overreaction with Q2 2026 earnings (late July) as the catalyst where any guidance raise will reprice the stock. Bear case: flat FY2026 guidance signals macro headwinds (rate sensitivity, consumer credit stress), and GAAP profitability still not established.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Three consecutive sell-the-beat quarters + Muddy Waters short report; beat-then-down pattern is structural until macro rotation favors growth fintech again

Bear case: Microsoft competition threatening e-signature moat, revenue growth stalled at ~8% YoY — may be a value trap.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft competition in document signing is structural; revenue growth stalled; IAM (Intelligent Agreement Management) pivot unproven; value trap risk without clear re-acceleration catalyst
